channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harris campaigns with Liz Cheney at the GOP's birthplace while Trump rallies in Michigan
item.description: RIPON, Wis. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris rallied with Republican Liz Cheney in the birthplace of the modern Republican Party on Thursday as the pair delivered a double-barreled denunciation of GOP nominee Donald Trump as a dire threat to democracy.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Peru celebrates 2 decades of a fast-growing breed of guinea pigs eaten as a delicacy
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru on Thursday celebrated two decades since the creation of a genetically modified breed of guinea pig, a rodent whose meat has formed a part of the diet of people in the Andean nation for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Garth Brooks accused of rape in lawsuit from hair-and-makeup artist
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A woman who says she worked as a hair-and-makeup stylist for Garth Brooks alleged in a lawsuit filed Thursday that he raped her in a Los Angeles hotel in 2019. The woman does not use her name and goes by Jane Roe in the lawsuit filed in Los Angeles Superior Court.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UN peacekeepers stay on Lebanon's border despite Israeli ground incursion
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — U.N. peacekeepers are staying in their positions on Lebanon’s southern border despite Israel’s request to vacate some areas before it launched its ground operation against Hezbollah militants, the U.N. peacekeeping chief said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Eight city workers arrested in Columbus Animal Care & Control investigation
item.description: "This investigation also uncovered multiple criminal violations that are part of the Georgia State Code," Deputy Police Chief Lance Deaton said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah James, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Texas couple raffling ACL tickets to pay for costly endometriosis surgery
item.description: Cole Stepro first noticed symptoms in December 2023 and started a 10-month journey with 17 doctors across the country.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Girtman, NewsNation

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man faces charges after he called 911 on someone who lit a scented candle: police
item.description: A New Jersey man is facing charges after fabricating both mental health and fire concerns over someone lighting a scented candle indoors, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 ex-officers convicted in fatal beating of Tyre Nichols
item.description: Former Memphis police officers Tadarrius Bean, Demetrius Haley and Justin Smith were convicted Thursday in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but cleared of the most serious charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firefighters union won't endorse in Trump-Harris race
item.description: The International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) announced Thursday it would not endorse a candidate in this year's presidential race, becoming the second major union to do so in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they are reviewing new evidence in the case of the Menendez brothers to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Motorized wheelchair stuffed with cocaine seized at Bush airport, CBP said
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) -- Unusually large cushions on a motorized wheelchair piqued the interest of officers at George Bush Intercontinental Airport last month in Houston, U.S. Customs and Border Protection said. Their suspicions were right.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to 'reevaluate' Menendez brothers' convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón addressed the public Thursday amid calls for his office to reexamine the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were found guilty of killing their parents in August 1989. Gascón said his office plans to do just that.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: NYC accelerates school leadership change as investigations swirl around mayor’s indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City is speeding up its switch to a new schools chief as indicted Mayor Eric Adams faces mounting pressure to bring stability to a city government that has been roiled by searches, subpoenas and resignations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The flood of ghost guns is slowing after regulation. It's also being challenged in the Supreme Court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Guy Boyd was hanging out with friends he had known for years in Ann Arbor, Michigan, on the night an accidental gunshot tore into his head.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Kirk strengthens into a Category 3 storm in the Atlantic
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Hurricane Kirk strengthened Wednesday into a Category 3 storm in the Atlantic Ocean and was expected to grow rapidly into a major hurricane, forecasters said. There were no coastal watches or warnings in effect, and the storm system was not yet deemed a threat to land.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shoppers are panic-buying toilet paper amid port strike, but experts say there’s no need
item.description: “Hope you have some toilet paper stock piled. It’s started. Sam’s and Costco are depleted,” one user said on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Pilot flying Helene rescue missions in NC ordered out, threatened with arrest
item.description: The fire official told him to tell any other pilots he knew they would also be arrested if they came back.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, ABC 4

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, KRON4

item.title: US 'Welcome Corps' helps resettle LGBTQ+ refugees fleeing crackdowns against gay people
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Cabrel Ngounou's life in Cameroon quickly unraveled after neighbors caught the teenager with his boyfriend. A crowd surrounded his boyfriend's house and beat him. Ngounou's family learned of the relationship and kicked him out.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and TERRY CHEA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New details released about inmate's escape in Northern California
item.description: Caleb Duerr, a 39-year-old inmate who escaped from the Plumas County Jail on September 27, was able to manipulate two fenced areas and flee to an O'Reilly's auto parts store.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Jennifer Boehme grew up scouting beaches around her home in St. Petersburg, Florida, for whatever she could find. Rocks, sand dollars, coquina mollusks — anything the ocean gave up. Now, 40 years later, Boehme wants to launch another treasure hunt.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment Tuesday against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, Fox 5

item.title: NYC mayor deflects questions about bribery charges as a potential witness speaks outside City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his most thorough public defense to date since his indictment on federal bribery charges, insisting the case was a “test” that would not distract him from governing as he deflected specific questions about the investigation for more than an ho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
